steps.title: Use the sex-specific prediction
steps.content: Ehmann and colleagues predicted that immature females would devote more attention than immature males to permanent residents of their home region. The reason is sex-specific: females typically remain in the same area from youth through adulthood, while males do not. Supporting data must therefore compare female and male peering proportions.

steps.title: Compare the proportions
steps.content: A gives the needed pattern: individuals 6 and 1 have higher proportions of peering toward permanent residents than individuals 13 and 15. B gives only the overall range, not a sex comparison. C compares within each sex rather than between sexes. D uses total peering events, but the hypothesis is about the proportion directed at permanent residents. The answer is A.
